title: orchis simia var. undulatifolia
intro: Orchis simia var. undulatifolia, also known as the monkey orchid, is a type of orchid species that belongs to the Orchidaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of China. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammatory conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The health benefits of Orchis simia var. undulatifolia include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting cardiovascular health, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a tea, and its tubers are also used to make dried powders or pills for medicinal purposes.
